title: fragaria vesca f. bracteata
intro: Fragaria vesca f. bracteata, also known as the woodland strawberry or alpine strawberry, is a plant species in the rose family that is characterized by its smaller size and unique bracteate inflorescence compared to the common strawberry. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Fragaria vesca f. bracteata include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to protect against certain types of cancer and neurodegenerative diseases. Fragaria vesca f. bracteata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
